channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nearly 900 Indians evacuated from Iran through Armenia and Azerbaijan amid ongoing conflict
item.description: Around 900 Indian nationals, the majority of them students from Jammu and Kashmir, have recently moved out of Iran through neighbouring Armenia and Azerbaijan due to the ongoing conflict triggered by ...

item.title: University of the Philippines tops 2026 EduRank list of 100 best PH universities
item.description: The University of the Philippines (UP) has once again taken the top position in the 2026 EduRank list of the Top 100 Philippine Universities. EduRank, a global university ranking system that evaluates...

item.title: MaXhosa Africa to showcase SIYI’KULTURE Autumn-Winter 2026 collection at MXS Kulture Festival in Johannesburg
item.description: MaXhosa Africa is set to present its highly anticipated Autumn-Winter 2026 collection, titled SIYI’KULTURE meaning “We Are Culture”, at the fourth edition of the MXS Kulture Festival on Saturday, 21 M...

item.title: Donald Trump Jokes About a United Ireland During St. Patrick’s Day Meeting Amid Rising International Tensions
item.description: During a St. Patrick’s Day visit to the White House, President Donald Trump made a controversial joke about a potential merger between Northern Ireland and the Republic of Ireland. The remark came whi...

item.title: Tanzania Explains Personal and Business Income Tax Structure
item.description: In Tanzania, personal income taxes are regulated under the Income Tax Act of 2004 with different rates and thresholds. The Pay-As-You-Earn (PAYE) system applies to salaries, wages, and allowances, wit...

item.title: Crowne Plaza expands meeting facilities to support Tanzania’s business tourism growth
item.description: Crowne Plaza Dar es Salaam has officially launched new meeting facilities designed to increase the city’s ability to host both local and international business events. The launch event brought togethe...

item.title: Consistent, Simple Strength Training Routines Proven Effective by New Guidelines
item.description: A major update to resistance training guidelines highlights a clear and practical message: consistency matters more than complexity. Based on 137 systematic reviews involving over 30,000 participants,...

item.title: Tracking Deer Movements in Ontario to Understand Ecology and Genetics
item.description: In Ontario’s Thousand Islands National Park, researchers from Trent University and Parks Canada are conducting an in-depth study of white-tailed deer to better understand their movement patterns and g...

item.title: Shawwal moon not sighted in Pakistan; Eidul Fitr set for Saturday, March 21
item.description: Central Ruet‑i‑Hilal Committee ne Pakistan mein Shawwal ki chand ki roshni dekhne ki koi sachi gawahi milti, is liye Eidul Fitr 21 March (Saturday) ko manaay jayrahe hain. Committee ka chairman Maulan...

item.title: Podcast discussion examines the rise of One Nation and its impact on Australian politics
item.description: This podcast episode from The Guardian’s Full Story series explores the growing influence of Pauline Hanson’s One Nation party in Australian politics and why it is attracting increasing support from v...

item.title: Intoxicated Woman Causes Traffic Disruption Outside Gujarat Hospital
item.description: According to reports, a visibly intoxicated woman created a major disturbance outside a hospital in Gujarat, leading to temporary traffic disruption. Eyewitnesses and commuters indicated that the woma...

item.title: US-Based Kenyan Woman Becomes Mother at 45 After Long Wait
item.description: Suzzie Blessed, a Kenyan woman living in the United States, has finally realised her dream of becoming a mother after waiting 45 years. She gave birth to her first child, a baby boy named Baraka Waith...

item.title: Erling Haaland invests in global chess tour to promote the game
item.description: Manchester City striker Erling Haaland has confirmed his investment in a new global chess tour, aiming to popularise chess among a wider audience. The tour, organised by Norway Chess, will launch next...

item.title: Homeowners face higher costs and interest rate pressures amid global uncertainty
item.description: South African homeowners are bracing for the combined impact of rising fuel prices, increased living costs, and potential interest rate pressures. Tensions between Iran and the United States are causi...

item.title: Pakistan rejects US concerns over its missile capabilities, calls them inaccurate and India-focused
item.description: Pakistan’s Foreign Office (FO) has strongly rejected recent remarks made by US Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, who claimed that Pakistan’s evolving missile capabilities could pose a t...

item.title: Veteran Filipino journalist Joel Pablo Salud passes away at 62 due to heart attack
item.description: Veteran journalist and author Joel Pablo Salud passed away on March 19, 2026, at the age of 62 after suffering a heart attack. His wife, journalist and author Che Sarigumba, confirmed the sad news to ...

item.title: Congress Announces Third List of Candidates for Assam Assembly Elections
item.description: On 19th March 2026, the Congress party in Assam unveiled its third list of candidates for the upcoming elections to the 126-seat Assam Legislative Assembly. The new list comprises 22 names, raising th...

item.title: Cape Town Nears Completion of Khayelitsha Sewer Line Repairs After Sinkhole Crisis
item.description: Repairs to a major sewer line in Khayelitsha, Cape Town, are finally nearing completion after months of disruption and health risks caused by collapsing pipes and sinkholes. The City of Cape Town has ...

item.title: Two Australians each win $5 million in Powerball draw #1557
item.description: In the latest Powerball draw #1557, two lucky punters each won a staggering $5 million, splitting the $10 million jackpot. The winning numbers for the draw were 18, 35, 22, 25, 4, 9, and 20, with the ...

item.title: Senate committee advances Markwayne Mullin’s nomination for Homeland Security despite bipartisan opposition
item.description: A U.S. Senate committee has narrowly advanced the nomination of Senator Markwayne Mullin to become Secretary of Homeland Security, sending his candidacy to a full Senate vote. The decision followed an...
